description_long: This object is a vintage console television with a large, boxy wooden cabinet and a prominent convex glass screen. The front features a dark, wood-grain finish with metallic trim, three rotary control knobs, and a speaker grille below the screen. The back panel is adorned with gold-embossed branding and ventilation slots, indicating the model as a 'Radiola Television' by 'Deep Image.' The sides and top are finished in a rich, dark wood veneer, giving it a classic mid-20th-century appearance. The television stands on four slender legs, elevating it slightly above the ground. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, with a strong retro aesthetic typical of 1950s-1960s home appliances.
description: A vintage wooden console television with a convex glass screen, metallic trim, and rotary control knobs, standing on four legs. The back features gold-embossed branding and ventilation slots.
description_view_0: The front view shows the convex glass screen, three rotary knobs, a speaker grille, and metallic trim, all set in a dark wood cabinet.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the boxy, wood-grain finish and the depth of the cabinet, with no visible controls or branding.
description_view_2: The rear view displays gold-embossed branding, ventilation slots, and technical information, all on a dark wood panel.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the wood-grain finish and the cabinet's depth, with no controls or branding visible.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, dark wood bezel with metallic trim, rectangular shape, and a classic mid-century style.
attribute_control_button_layout: Three rotary knobs in a horizontal row below the screen on the front panel.
